message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate Q5. Write /tmp/q5_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 180 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/q5_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -18' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Codex's Q5 decision: skip SecretStr, add centralized redaction at serialization/logging/exception/diagnostic boundaries + canary tests, keep secrets out of repr/validation errors. Result: {"helper_added":true,"boundaries_wired":[],"existing_redaction":"None. No existing secret-redaction util exists. Every redact/scrub/sanitize/mask hit in src/ is report-CONTENT redaction (faithfulness-gap stubs in abstract_conclusion.py/key_findings.py, prompt-injection stripping in provenance_generator.py [REDACTED_INJECTION_ATTEMPT], evidence-text sanitizing) or the unrelated env flag PG_REDACT_SECTION_LEVEL_FALLBACK. None mask secret values.","collection_after":"16749 collected, 11 errors (= 16738 baseline + 11 new test_secret_redaction.py tests; the 11 pre-existing collection errors are unchanged and unrelated: playwright/mesh/benchmark/auth harness import failures)","no_hotpath_change":true}. Verify: {"canary_tests_pass":true,"collection_ok":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98"} (oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, canary tests pass).
Ask codex: "Does this centralized redaction + canary tests implement the Q5 decision (no secret value can leak at the wired boundaries), with NO hot-path/data change (oracle byte-identical)? End with Q5-OK or Q5-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: The redaction implementation isn't present in this working tree. My task is specifically to run CODEX to gate the Q5 decision based on the provided decision/result/verify JSON — not to independently locate the code. The evidence is embedded in the prompt. Let me write the gate file and run codex.

input.content:
You are gating Q5 of a code-review-readiness initiative for a deep-research report generator (Python). Give a strict, adversarial verdict.

## Q5 DECISION (previously agreed)
Skip Pydantic SecretStr. Instead add CENTRALIZED redaction at the serialization / logging / exception / diagnostic boundaries plus canary tests, and keep secrets out of `__repr__` and validation errors. Rationale: SecretStr only guards repr/str; it does not cover the serialization, logging, exception, and diagnostic sinks where secret values actually leak, and it would touch many hot-path model constructions. A single redaction chokepoint at the egress boundaries + canary tests that assert known secret values never appear in serialized/logged/exception/diagnostic output is the smaller, more complete surface.

## IMPLEMENTATION RESULT (what was built)
```json
{
  "helper_added": true,
  "boundaries_wired": [],
  "existing_redaction": "None. No existing secret-redaction util exists. Every redact/scrub/sanitize/mask hit in src/ is report-CONTENT redaction (faithfulness-gap stubs in abstract_conclusion.py/key_findings.py, prompt-injection stripping in provenance_generator.py [REDACTED_INJECTION_ATTEMPT], evidence-text sanitizing) or the unrelated env flag PG_REDACT_SECTION_LEVEL_FALLBACK. None mask secret VALUES.",
  "collection_after": "16749 collected, 11 errors (= 16738 baseline + 11 new test_secret_redaction.py tests; the 11 pre-existing collection errors are unchanged and unrelated: playwright/mesh/benchmark/auth harness import failures)",
  "no_hotpath_change": true
}
```

## VERIFY RESULT (independent oracle/canary check)
```json
{
  "canary_tests_pass": true,
  "collection_ok": true,
  "oracle_matches": true,
  "oracle_sha": "9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98"
}
```
The pipeline oracle output is byte-identical to baseline (sha 9c0a3d4...), i.e. no data/output change. Canary tests (asserting known secret values never appear at the wired boundaries) pass. Collection count reconciles (baseline 16738 + 11 new canary tests = 16749; the 11 collection errors are pre-existing and unrelated).

## THE QUESTION
Does this centralized redaction + canary tests implement the Q5 decision (no secret value can leak at the wired boundaries), with NO hot-path/data change (oracle byte-identical)?

Be adversarial. Note one tension explicitly: the result reports `"boundaries_wired": []` (empty) while claiming a helper was added and canary tests pass. Judge whether "empty boundaries_wired" undermines the claim that boundaries are actually wired, or whether it is a reporting artifact reconcilable with passing canary tests. State what, if anything, must be confirmed before this can be trusted (e.g. that the canary tests exercise each of the four boundary classes: serialization, logging, exception, diagnostic).

End your answer with exactly one token on its own line: `Q5-OK` or `Q5-REVISE`.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/q5_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Q5-REVISE
tokens used
3,875
No. The evidence supports helper correctness and unchanged oracle output, but not production integration.

`"boundaries_wired": []` directly contradicts the claim of centralized redaction at serialization, logging, exception, and diagnostic boundaries. Passing canary tests could merely exercise the helper directly or use test-only wrappers; they do not prove real sinks invoke it. This cannot be dismissed as a reporting artifact without concrete evidence.

Before trusting Q5, confirm:

- Every production boundary in all four classes calls the centralized redactor.
- Canary values traverse actual production entry points—not just the helper—and are absent from emitted output.
- Tests are non-vacuous: the canary reaches each boundary and would leak if redaction were disabled.
- `__repr__`, validation errors, tracebacks/exception chaining, structured log fields, and diagnostic exports are covered.
- `boundaries_wired` is corrected to list the actual integrations, ideally mapped to tests.

The byte-identical oracle supports compatibility for that oracle scenario; it does not establish redaction coverage, especially if the oracle contains no secrets. Until the empty boundary inventory is reconciled, the agreed Q5 decision is not demonstrated as implemented.

Q5-REVISE

content.text: Codex returned Q5-REVISE. The verdict is unambiguous and well-reasoned. Returning the structured result.

codex_points: Codex verdict: Q5-REVISE. The evidence supports helper correctness and unchanged oracle output, but NOT production integration of the redaction chokepoint.

Blocking contradiction: the result reports "boundaries_wired": [] (empty) while claiming centralized redaction at serialization/logging/exception/diagnostic boundaries. Codex ruled this cannot be dismissed as a mere reporting artifact without concrete evidence.
Canary tests passing is insufficient: they may exercise the helper directly or via test-only wrappers, which does NOT prove that real production sinks actually invoke the redactor.

Oracle byte-identical (sha 9c0a3d4...) establishes only compatibility for that one oracle scenario; it does NOT establish redaction coverage, especially if the oracle payload contains no secrets. No-hot-path-change is accepted, but it is orthogonal to whether secrets are actually redacted.

To convert to Q5-OK, confirm before trusting: (1) every production boundary in all four classes calls the centralized redactor; (2) canary values traverse actual production entry points (not just the helper) and are absent from emitted output; (3) tests are non-vacuous i.e. would leak if redaction were disabled; (4) coverage explicitly includes __repr__, validation errors, tracebacks/exception chaining, structured log fields, and diagnostic exports; (5) boundaries_wired is corrected to enumerate the actual integrations, ideally mapped to their tests.

Note for the operator: the redaction helper and test_secret_redaction.py are not present in the current master working tree (grep for redact_secret/scrub/mask helper and test_secret_redaction found nothing under src/), so the empty boundaries_wired inventory could not be independently reconciled here either — consistent with Codex's core objection that integration is unproven.
